changes.field: wave1ConfidenceMarkerDisplayLabel utility read — incomplete coverage for CV and blood sugar markers
changes.finding: CANONICAL_ID_TO_LABEL in wave1ConfidenceMarkerLabels.ts covers only 10 liver-related markers (alt, ast, ggt, alp, alkaline_phosphatase, albumin, globulin, total_protein, total_bilirubin, bilirubin). All CV and blood sugar subsystem markers fall through to the generic fallback: split on underscore, titlecase each part. This produces 'Total Cholesterol' (acceptable), but 'Ldl Cholesterol', 'Hdl Cholesterol', 'Tc Hdl Ratio' (poor), and single-word markers with no underscore are returned as-is without capitalisation: 'homocysteine', 'crp', 'glucose', 'hba1c', 'insulin' (all lower-case, unacceptable in consumer UI). Cursor must extend this utility or create a new subsystem-marker display label function covering all 13 expected subsystem marker IDs.
citations: frontend/app/lib/wave1ConfidenceMarkerLabels.ts lines 5-16: CANONICAL_ID_TO_LABEL — 10 liver markers only — CONFIRMED
frontend/app/lib/wave1ConfidenceMarkerLabels.ts lines 23-28: fallback — split underscore, no capitalisation for single-word ids — CONFIRMED

changes.field: GATE-BLOCKING CONFLICT 1 — UX1A Sentinel test asserts 'subsystem' not in Wave1DomainCards.tsx
changes.finding: backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1a_wave1_health_systems_card_scaffold.py line 141 asserts: assert 'subsystem' not in cards.lower(). This is a case-insensitive substring match. The DTO field d.subsystems contains the substring 'subsystem'. Any reference to d.subsystems, sub.subsystem_id, sub.subsystem_label, or a prop named subsystems in Wave1DomainCards.tsx will cause this test to FAIL and the Gate will reject the sprint. This guard was written to prevent placeholder subsystem UI; it must now be updated by UX1D to permit governed rendering. Required action: Cursor must update this sentinel test BEFORE adding any subsystem reference to Wave1DomainCards.tsx. The updated assertion must still guard against frontend-invented subsystem labels and placeholder UI, but must permit the governed rendering introduced by UX1D. The updated test should reference the new UX1D regression test file as co-authority.
citations: backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1a_wave1_health_systems_card_scaffold.py line 137-141: test_health_system_card_subsystem_placeholder_visible_sentinel — assert 'subsystem' not in cards.lower() — CONFIRMED will fail

changes.field: GATE-BLOCKING CONFLICT 2 — UX1C Sentinel test asserts 'included_marker_ids' not in Wave1DomainCards.tsx
changes.finding: backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py line 139 asserts: assert 'included_marker_ids' not in cards. Lines 129-130 additionally assert: assert 'Lipid transport' not in cards and assert 'wave1_cv_lipid_transport' not in cards (all read from Wave1DomainCards.tsx). These guards prevent frontend-calculated marker groupings and hardcoded subsystem labels. They remain valid in principle but will conflict with UX1D if subsystem rendering is placed inline in Wave1DomainCards.tsx. Required action: Cursor MUST put all subsystem rendering in a dedicated sub-component (e.g. Wave1SubsystemSection.tsx). Wave1DomainCards.tsx passes the subsystems array via prop but contains none of the guarded strings directly. The UX1D regression test then scopes its own guards to the sub-component file. Note: even with a sub-component, the prop pass-through in Wave1DomainCards.tsx will contain 'subsystem' (from conflict 1 above) — both conflicts must be resolved together.
changes.resolution: REQUIRED — dedicated sub-component isolation mandatory. Inline rendering will fail the Gate.
citations: backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py line 129: assert 'Lipid transport' not in cards — CONFIRMED will fail if inline
backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py line 130: assert 'wave1_cv_lipid_transport' not in cards — CONFIRMED will fail if inline
backend/tests/regression/test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py line 139: assert 'included_marker_ids' not in cards — CONFIRMED will fail if inline

changes.field: wave1ConfidenceMarkerDisplayLabel must be extended for subsystem marker display
changes.finding: The existing utility in wave1ConfidenceMarkerLabels.ts covers only 10 liver markers. All 13 CV and blood sugar subsystem expected markers (total_cholesterol, ldl_cholesterol, hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides, tc_hdl_ratio, homocysteine, crp, glucose, hba1c, insulin and the liver markers already covered) will produce poor output from the generic fallback for markers without underscores (homocysteine, crp, glucose, hba1c, insulin returned as-is, lower-case) and misleading titlecase for acronym markers (Ldl Cholesterol, Hdl Cholesterol, Tc Hdl Ratio). Cursor must extend CANONICAL_ID_TO_LABEL or create a parallel subsystem display label function covering all expected marker IDs before rendering subsystem sections in the UI.
changes.resolution: REQUIRED for acceptable consumer presentation. Acceptable in wave1ConfidenceMarkerLabels.ts (extend the existing map) or in a new utility under frontend/app/lib/.
citations: frontend/app/lib/wave1ConfidenceMarkerLabels.ts lines 5-16: CANONICAL_ID_TO_LABEL — liver markers only — CONFIRMED
backend/core/analytics/wave1_subsystem_evidence.py lines 25-78: full marker id set that needs display labels — CONFIRMED

changes.field: Sentinel near-duplicate — fake_status_visible vs fake_status_emitted can coexist
changes.finding: Prompt requires new class 'health_system_subsystem_fake_status_visible' (frontend renders a status not supplied by backend). UX1C has 'health_system_subsystem_fake_status_emitted' at line 760 (backend emits status without safe derivation). These guard different failure modes and can coexist. No conflict.
changes.resolution: Non-blocking — two distinct classes are legitimate.
